theme: The myth of the 'safe' domestic event (and what the numbers say)

linkedin.text:
Every year, budget pressure pushes the same conversation:

"We should keep the incentive trip domestic this time."

I want to challenge that — not with an opinion, but with what we've actually observed across 1,500+ events and 25,000+ participants over 16 years.

The assumption: local = cost-effective, international = risky indulgence.
The reality: domestic programmes routinely underdeliver on the one metric that makes incentive travel worth the investment — the sense of being genuinely set apart from the everyday.

Here's the mechanism: the act of travelling together — clearing security, landing somewhere unfamiliar, navigating a new city as a group — creates psychological distance from the office dynamic. That distance is what allows real connection, real recognition, real reset.

A venue your team can drive to on a Tuesday doesn't create that. No matter how good the catering is.

We've run incentive programmes across 130+ destinations, from Iceland to Japan to the Seychelles, for teams of 20 to 2,000. The ROI conversation almost always looks different after they land than it did before they booked.

If you're currently building the business case for an international incentive programme — and need data points to support it internally — DM me. This is a conversation worth having before the budget gets locked.

instagram.text:
"Let's play it safe and keep it local this year."

We've heard this before almost every standout incentive trip we've ever produced.

Here's what the pattern actually shows after 1,500+ events in 130+ destinations:

The "safe" choice costs more per memorable moment. The domestic venue that's easy to get to is easy to forget. And the top performers your programme is designed to reward can feel the difference between valued and managed.

Distance is part of the signal. Getting on a plane together rewrites the team dynamic in ways a local dinner never does.

25,000+ participants. The events they still talk about were never the ones closest to home. ✈️

From business to pleasure — that's not just a destination change. It's a message.

DM us if you're weighing up what your next incentive really needs to achieve.

#IncentiveTravel #CorporateEvents #MICE #TeamRetreat #EmployeeRecognition #B2BEvents #EventStrategy #UproductionEvents

facebook.text:
The most common thing we hear from HR and marketing leaders before they sign off on an incentive trip:

"Let's play it safe and keep it local this year."

We understand the instinct. Budgets are tighter, timelines are compressed, stakeholder scrutiny is real.

But here's what 16 years and 1,500+ events across 130+ destinations actually shows:

The "safe" local event almost always costs more per lasting impression than an international incentive designed right. The logistics look simpler until they're not. The wow factor — the thing that makes a top performer feel genuinely valued — rarely survives a venue your team drives to on a Tuesday.

Distance is part of the signal. Getting on a plane together changes the social contract.

25,000+ participants across our events. The ones people still talk about? They almost never happened down the road.

Happy to share how we've made international programmes work inside realistic budgets. DM us or comment below. 👇

image_prompt: Aerial drone photograph of a long banquet table set for 200 people on a wide white-sand beach framed by palm trees and a vivid turquoise lagoon, guests in business-casual attire beginning to take their seats as waitstaff in white linen move between them, golden late-afternoon light casting long shadows across the sand.
